verses.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; Whom should I fear? The LORD is the defense of my life; Whom should I dread?
verses.2: When evildoers came upon me to devour my flesh, My adversaries and my enemies, they stumbled and fell.
verses.3: If an army encamps against me, My heart will not fear; If war arises against me, In spite of this I am confident.
verses.4: One thing I have asked from the LORD, that I shall seek: That I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, To behold the beauty of the LORD And to meditate in His temple.
verses.5: For on the day of trouble He will conceal me in His tabernacle; He will hide me in the secret place of His tent; He will lift me up on a rock.
verses.6: And now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me, And I will offer sacrifices in His tent with shouts of joy; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the LORD.
verses.7: Hear, LORD, when I cry with my voice, And be gracious to me and answer me.
verses.8: When You said, “Seek My face,” my heart said to You, “I shall seek Your face, LORD.”
verses.9: Do not hide Your face from me, Do not turn Your servant away in anger; You have been my help; Do not abandon me nor forsake me, God of my salvation!
verses.10: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, But the LORD will take me up.
verses.11: Teach me Your way, LORD, And lead me on a level path Because of my enemies.
verses.12: Do not turn me over to the desire of my enemies, For false witnesses have risen against me, And the violent witness.
verses.13: I certainly believed that I would see the goodness of the LORD In the land of the living.
verses.14: Wait for the LORD; Be strong and let your heart take courage; Yes, wait for the LORD.
